name: Cranberry Fruit Extract
historicalUse: North American cranberries were used by indigenous peoples for urinary conditions and wound healing long before European contact. Early American colonial medicine adopted cranberry preparations for urinary and kidney complaints. By the 19th century, cranberry-based preparations appeared in formal pharmacopoeias. The anti-adhesion mechanism for urinary bacteria was not understood until the late 20th century, but the empirical use was centuries ahead of the mechanism.
modernEvidence: PAC-standardized cranberry extract (proanthocyanidins type A) prevents uropathogenic E. coli from adhering to urothelial cell surfaces via P-fimbriae inhibition. This anti-adhesion mechanism — not acidification of urine as previously believed — is the validated mechanism. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy in reducing UTI recurrence in prone individuals. 40:1 extract provides consistent PAC dosing.
mechanisms: P-fimbriae inhibition — type A PACs prevent bacterial adherence to uroepithelial glycoproteins
Anti-biofilm — reduces bacterial biofilm formation in the urinary tract
Antioxidant — anthocyanins reduce oxidative stress in bladder mucosa
Acidification (minor) — mild urinary acidification contributes to bacteriostatic environment
benefits: Supports urinary tract comfort and health
Supports immune response in the urinary tract
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Well tolerated. High-dose concentrated extract may cause mild GI discomfort.
contraindications: Caution in patients with history of calcium oxalate kidney stones — cranberry increases urinary oxalate
drugInteractions: Warfarin — case reports of increased anticoagulant effect; monitor INR
CYP2C9 substrates — cranberry may mildly inhibit CYP2C9; clinical significance at extract doses unclear
physicianNotes: PAC type A standardization is essential — type B PACs (in grape seed, pine bark) do not demonstrate anti-adhesion activity. Appropriate as prophylaxis in women with recurrent UTI. Not a treatment for active UTI — refer for appropriate antimicrobial therapy if infection is confirmed.
patientGuidance: This formulation helps prevent bacteria from adhering to the bladder wall. It is a preventive support — if you have symptoms of an active urinary tract infection (burning, frequency, fever), see your physician for proper treatment.
